corrections.finding: review-gate HIGH: section 1 (world-model-rules-engine.md:16, :19, :27) states 'The kernel's formal machinery does nothing', names 'the root cause', and asserts 'Nothing in the estate materialises entailments' together with a list of missing components ('a parent relation, a person class, a covering axiom, a materialisation pass').
corrections.phrase_superseded: 'nothing in the estate materialises entailments' and the missing-components list, read as current repo-state claims; and the untagged causal 'root cause' framing read beyond DECONF-A1's scope
corrections.binding_reading: These sentences were true when drafted (the doc is the RULES-1 preregistration input and PREDATES the RULES-1 build) and are SUPERSEDED as repo-state claims by the frozen RULES-1 build: data/axioms-kinship-v1/ exists (8 axiom records: chain-grandfather, chain-grandmother, chain-grandparent, class-person, cover-parent, rel-parent, sub-father-parent, sub-mother-parent; plus 5 concept records: grandfather, grandmother, grandparent, parent, person), a materializer is present (sparq-reason closure engine + poc/rules-1/certificate.py, twin_engine.py, rules1_runner.py), and registry/experiments/rules-1.json is FROZEN with the CPU certificate rerun pinned (stated C_dec 1.0 = 1716/1716; entailed C_dec 0.0 = 0/3680 stated-projection-reproducible; CLUTRR 858/858, Wilson-LB95 0.9955). Separately, DECONF-A1 establishes extensional decision-equivalence only for its pinned answer-check-resample topology and read-set; it does not establish that all formal machinery does nothing, nor causally establish engine absence as 'the root cause' - the machinery-does-nothing sentence is bounded to the audit's mechanical sense and the root-cause sentence is design-level interpretation, not a measured causal claim.
applies_to: all forward narration quoting the 'machinery does nothing' / 'root cause' / 'nothing materialises entailments' framing

temporal_snapshot_ruling: DECISION: docs/next/arch/world-model-rules-engine.md is ruled a TEMPORAL SNAPSHOT - the preregistration-time design record whose exact bytes the FROZEN rules-1.json pins. It is intentionally NOT edited (no conspicuous inline markings added), because any byte change breaks the prereg_doc sha pin of a FROZEN registration. Readers obtain the current state from: (1) the doc's own amendment notice for design deltas, (2) this correction record for repo-state staleness, (3) registry/experiments/rules-1.json + poc/rules-1/ artifacts for the built system. Successor architecture documents must cite the doc AS a snapshot with this record alongside.
non_retroactivity: No frozen record, verdict, certificate, results log, or registered assumption changes. The RULES-1 freeze, its pins, and its claim caps (machinery non-inertness only; kernel-specific value, host lift, NL reach, and efficiency all pending) are untouched. This record narrows forward READING only.
